hero.question: Will Apple be the second-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31?
hero.prob_take: The 93% consensus basically prices in Apple staying ahead of Saudi Aramco and staying put in the $3T+ club, which is the base case unless the market tanks hard between now and March. The real edge is watching whether Nvidia's rally stalls or if a rate-cut reversal hammers growth stocks, both scenarios that could shuffle the top 3 by year-end.

daily_take.headline: Apple's Coronation as #2 is Already Priced In, Saudi Money Says Otherwise
daily_take.deck: Apple jumped 3.5 points to 93% odds of being the world's second-largest company by March 31, riding the AI rally and renewed confidence in Big Tech dominance. But the math doesn't require heroics here. Microsoft, Saudi Aramco, and Alphabet would all need to collapse or Apple to rocket another $500B in value for this to fail. Smart money isn't buying certainty at 93 cents on the dollar. Shorts have value if you believe mean reversion hits tech before Q1 ends.

sidebar.headline: Steyer's gubernatorial odds spiked 4.2 points on zero news. Someone's front-running information or chasing narrative. Worth watching the California money flows.
sidebar.label: Tom Steyer 2026: 7.2%

sidebar.headline: Mavericks surged 5 points to 27.5% against the Bucks. Check if this reflects a shift in Vegas lines or just retail chasing Dallas momentum after their recent run.
sidebar.label: Mavericks Moneyline: 27.5%

sidebar.headline: Anduril IPO collapsed 22 points in a day. Defense contractors don't crater on sentiment alone. Either funding talks stalled or someone knows the timeline just stretched beyond 2027.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO 2027: 28.0%
